title: Charge & Saturation
subtitle: The acute bill and the chronic one

content.text: Power now. The body invoices later.

Rift toxicity runs on two meters. Charge is acute — the heat in the body right now, this fight, this day. Saturation is chronic — the career total. Charge you don't shed becomes Saturation; Saturation you don't drain becomes thresholds.
Charge — the Acute Meter (0–10)
Charge runs 0 to 10 and comes from four places:
Weaving — every Weave generates Charge (amounts — see Weave & Fire).
Leech Burns — the Leech tree runs its engine on them (see Leech).
Rift exposure — rift-surge nodes and similar hazards, as the hazard states.
Mend Weave — the patient pays for the heal in Charge (see Patterns).
Anything else adds Charge only when its own entry says so.
At Charge 7+: your Weaves carry misfire exposure — any Weave you fail misfires (roll on the Misfire Table — see Weave & Fire). Bootleg patterns open the window earlier (see Patterns).
At Charge 10: forced vent — you take 1d6 direct backlash, and all excess sticks.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The forced vent at 10 is canon as written — "1d6 backlash, excess sticks" — but underspecified: the docs never state how much Charge the forced vent clears, or whether the stuck excess stays as Charge or converts to Saturation.
Venting — the End-of-Encounter Bill

At an encounter's end, vent: shed Charge up to your Grit bonus for free (bonus table — see Stats & Growth). Every point past that converts into Saturation, 1:1. By the end of the vent your Charge is 0 — every point was either shed free or banked as Saturation.

content.label: AUGMENT HOOKS
content.text: The Channeler tree sells vent improvements and mid-fight Charge shedding — see the Channeler tree. The vent itself is free and automatic; the tree just makes it bigger.
Saturation — the Career Meter
Saturation accrues from unvented Charge and never falls on its own. Five thresholds, in order:

rows: You glow faintly under dome light. Corps notice.
Your free vent is reduced by 1.
Your next splice or mutation roll is at disadvantage; wild-graft interactions awaken.
Roll a permanent mutation, now (the roll and its outcomes — see Mutations & Grafts).
Off the player's sheet. What happens at 100 belongs to the GM and the setting.

content.text: Detox — the Drain

Detox is how Saturation comes down: paid care, priced by provider tier. The usual provider is a Street Doc (see Street Doc); the full provider ladder and its prices live with the GM's economy pages, not here.

Routine saturation screening is complimentary for all contracted personnel. An elevated reading is not a disciplinary event. Please proceed to your designated wellness provider before the glow becomes visible to clients.
